things.name: Independence Avenue (Praspiekt Niezaliezhnasci)
things.description: Grand Soviet-era boulevard with Stalinist architecture stretching 15km through the city

things.name: Independence Square
things.description: Vast central square dominated by the House of Government and the KGB headquarters

things.name: Mir Castle
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage 16th-century Gothic-Renaissance castle 100km from Minsk

things.name: Nesvizh Castle
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Radziwill family palace with formal gardens and frescoes

things.name: Great Patriotic War Museum
things.description: Belarus's largest museum commemorating WWII, with a remarkable star-shaped building

things.name: Trinity Suburb (Traetskae Pradmestse)
things.description: Minsk's restored 19th-century old town along the Svislach river

things.name: Island of Tears Memorial
things.description: Haunting memorial on a small island to soldiers who died in the Soviet-Afghan War

things.name: Minsk Metro Art
things.description: Soviet-era metro stations with elaborate mosaics, stained glass and socialist murals

things.name: National Art Museum of Belarus
things.description: Largest art museum in Belarus with 27,000 works including Belarusian and Russian masters

things.name: Brest Fortress
things.description: WWII fortress where Soviet soldiers held out against Nazi attack - a moving memorial
